rows.speaker: A
rows.english: When did you come from Delhi ?

rows.speaker: B
rows.english: I came last month.

rows.speaker: A
rows.english: How did you come ?

rows.speaker: B
rows.english: I came by plane from Delhi to
Madras.

rows.speaker: A
rows.english: How did you come here from there ?

rows.speaker: B
rows.english: I came by train from there.

rows.speaker: A
rows.english: How was the journey ?

rows.speaker: B
rows.english: Oh, it was awful.

rows.speaker: A
rows.english: Where did you go yesterday ?

rows.speaker: B
rows.english: We went to Nandi hills yesterday.

rows.speaker: A
rows.english: At what time did you leave ?

rows.speaker: B
rows.english: We started at five in the morning.

rows.speaker: A
rows.english: Did you get up so early ?

rows.speaker: B
rows.english: Yes, we got up at 4'o clock.

rows.speaker: A
rows.english: Did every body come by that time ?

rows.speaker: B
rows.english: Yes, just one friend came late.

rows.speaker: A
rows.english: What did you drink this morning ?

rows.speaker: B
rows.english: I drank milk this morning.

rows.speaker: A
rows.english: What snacks did you eat ?

rows.speaker: B
rows.roman: erad idli ond doose ond vade
rows.english: I ate two iddlies, one pancake and
one vade.

rows.speaker: A
rows.english: Did you bathe in the morning ?

rows.speaker: B
rows.english: Yes, I did.

rows.speaker: A
rows.english: Has today's paper come and did
you read it ?
